headline: From prime regularity exploration to generator-fingerprint research tooling.

change_dashboard.headline: PrimeProject moved from exploratory prime regularity visuals into publication-gated real-world generator fingerprint tooling.

maturity_ladder.signal: controlled attribution, null calibration, and 8-setting replication audit
OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin collection targets and sample-power floors
provenance, acceptance, and promotion gates before claims
5 falsification checks and controlled-synthetic-only claim floor

latest_changes.label: Baseline promotion plan
latest_changes.impact: Turns blocked baselines into a concrete OpenSSL/BoringSSL unlock path.

latest_changes.label: Baseline acceptance gate
latest_changes.impact: Prevents coarse or undocumented baselines from supporting attribution claims.

latest_changes.label: Provenance audit
latest_changes.impact: Checks missing metadata, checksum format, and forbidden public sensitive fields.

latest_changes.label: Evidence pack gates
latest_changes.impact: Bundles checksums and publication limits so GitHub Pages shows claim boundaries.

latest_changes.label: Claim ledger
latest_changes.impact: Maps public statements to gates so unsupported real-world and Bitcoin attribution claims stay blocked.

latest_changes.label: Artifact lineage
latest_changes.impact: Audits public JSON dependencies and evidence-pack checksums as an acyclic reproducibility graph.

latest_changes.label: Decision protocol
latest_changes.impact: Pre-registers promotion rules so demo, synthetic, real-world, and Bitcoin claims cannot drift after results.

latest_changes.label: Falsification battery
latest_changes.impact: Runs paired controls, negative controls, bit-length confound guards, and claim-promotion guards before stronger claims.

latest_changes.label: Null calibration
latest_changes.impact: Tests whether the best-looking controlled profile survives row-structured random-label simulation and multiple-profile selection.

latest_changes.label: Replication audit
latest_changes.impact: Checks whether null-calibrated profiles repeat across limit, train-count, and test-count settings instead of depending on one run.

phases.label: Prime regularity plan
phases.summary: Defined the practical target as generator traces and anomaly detection, not raw prime prediction.

phases.label: Interactive Conjecture Lab
phases.summary: Built browser-side comparison of rejection, next_prime, and wheel observation measures up to 10M.

phases.label: 10M research snapshots
phases.summary: Bundled local precomputed SVG snapshots so GitHub Pages can show larger runs without recomputing.

phases.label: Bitcoin defensive track
phases.summary: Separated public secp256k1 constants from practical nonce and signature metadata risk.

phases.label: Generator fingerprint baseline
phases.summary: Added residue, low-bit, and local prime-gap fingerprints with baseline distance comparison.

phases.label: Controlled attribution grid
phases.summary: Added paired uncontrolled and bit-length-controlled experiments with repeat confidence intervals.

phases.label: Null calibration
phases.summary: Calibrates controlled attribution profiles against a row-structured random-label null with family-wise profile selection control.

phases.label: Replication audit
phases.summary: Audits whether null-calibrated controlled profiles replicate across limit, train-count, and test-count settings.

phases.label: Real-world baseline registry
phases.summary: Registered OpenSSL, BoringSSL, Go, Bitcoin Core, and wallet/library families while keeping sensitive raw data local.

phases.label: Real-world collection matrix
phases.summary: Defines matched 2048/3072/4096-bit RSA and Bitcoin signature metadata targets before claims can advance.

phases.label: Sample power calibration
phases.summary: Estimates conservative TV-drift screening floors so sample counts are not treated as stronger evidence than they support.

phases.label: Provenance requirements
phases.summary: Defines required metadata and forbidden public fields before real-world samples can support attribution claims.

phases.label: Provenance audit
phases.summary: Audits filled provenance records for missing metadata, checksum format, and forbidden public sensitive fields before attribution claims.

phases.label: Baseline acceptance
phases.summary: Combines collection availability, provenance audit, and sample-power tier before a real-world baseline can be used for attribution claims.

phases.label: Promotion plan
phases.summary: Identifies the minimal OpenSSL/BoringSSL-style unlock path and required sample floor before accepted real-world evidence is possible.

phases.label: Research readiness scoring
phases.summary: Scores Sim-to-Real, attribution validation, classifier data, and Bitcoin integration before allowing stronger claims.

phases.label: Evidence pack gates
phases.summary: Bundles artifact checksums, publication gates, claim level, and local collection protocols.

phases.label: Claim ledger
phases.summary: Maps each public-facing claim to the gates and artifacts that allow, qualify, or block it.

phases.label: Artifact lineage
phases.summary: Builds an acyclic dependency graph over public artifacts and checks evidence-pack checksums against current files.

phases.label: Decision protocol
phases.summary: Applies pre-registered publication and claim-promotion rules to evidence, claim, and lineage reports.

phases.label: Falsification battery
phases.summary: Runs negative-control, bit-length confound, and claim-promotion checks before attribution claims can be upgraded.

open_gaps.gap: Need at least two available real-world aggregate baselines before real attribution claims.
Need labelled feature vectors across OpenSSL, BoringSSL, Go, and suspicious samples.
Need a bundled Bitcoin nonce-risk report from owned or public metadata summaries.
